subject: Chemistry
q: Which one among the following metals is the weakest reducing agent?
question: Which one among the following metals is the weakest reducing agent?
solution:
According to the electrochemical series

K+ / K → -2.93V

Rb+ / Rb → -2.98V

Na+ / Na → -2.71V

Li+ / Li → -3.05V

Among the given alkali metals, sodium metal is the weakest reducing agent as its standard reduction potential is least negative which is ENa+/Na° = -2.71

So, the correct option is C.

q: The electronegativity of aluminum is similar to:
question: The electronegativity of aluminum is similar to:
solution:
EN of Al=1.61

EN of Be=1.57

EN of B=2.04

EN of C=2.55

Due to diagonal relationship between Be and Al, they exhibit similar properties and thus, have similar electronegativity values.

q: Two elements <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math> have similar chemical properties. They don't form solid hydrogencarbonates, but react with nitrogen to form nitrides. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math>, respectively, are :

They don't form solid hydrogencarbonates, but react with nitrogen to form nitrides. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math>, respectively, are :
solution:
Li and Mg do not form solid bicarbonate. But react with N2 to give nitrides. Both of them have similar properties due to diagonal relationship.
6 Li+N2⟶Δ 2 Li3N
3 Mg+N2⟶ΔMg3N2

subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are two statements : one is labelled as Assertion (A) and the other is labelled as Reason (R).
Assertion (A): Lithium salts are hydrated.
Reason (R) : Lithium has higher polarising power than other alkali metal group members.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
question:
Given below are two statements : one is labelled as Assertion (A) and the other is labelled as Reason (R).
Assertion (A): Lithium salts are hydrated.
Reason (R) : Lithium has higher polarising power than other alkali metal group members.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
options: Both (A) and (R) are correct but (R) is NOT the correct explanation of (A).
Both (A) and (R) are correct and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
solution:
Li salts are hydrated eg.  LiCl. 6H2O, because lithium-ion has high hydration enthalpy.

Smaller the size of cation more is the polarising power.
Polarising power order:  Li+>Na+>K+> Rb+>Cs+

subject: Chemistry
q: Portland cement contains '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math>' to enhance the setting time. What is '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math>'?
question: Portland cement contains '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math>' to enhance the setting time. What is '<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math>'?
solution:
Cement is an important building material. It was first introduced in England in 1824 by Joseph Aspdin. It is also called Portland cement because it resembles with the natural limestone quarried in the Isle of Portland, England.

The raw materials for the manufacture of cement are limestone and clay.

When mixed with water, the setting of cement takes place to give a hard mass. This is due to the hydration of the molecules of the constituents and their rearrangement. The purpose of adding gypsum is only to slow down the process of setting of the cement so that it gets sufficiently hardened.

Hence,GypsumCaSO4·2H2O is used to enhance setting time in portland cement.

solution: Group 1 elements are most metallic, as we go across the periodic table metallic character increases while going down the group and decreases along the period. Silicon is p-block element, potassium, beryllium and magnesium are s-block elements.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are two statements, one is labelled as

Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R.

Assertion A : Beryllium has less negative value of reduction potential compared to the other alkaline earth metals

Reason R : Beryllium has large hydration energy due to small size of Be2+but relatively large value of atomisation enthalpy.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below.
question:
Given below are two statements, one is labelled as

Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R.

Assertion A : Beryllium has less negative value of reduction potential compared to the other alkaline earth metals

Reason R : Beryllium has large hydration energy due to small size of Be2+but relatively large value of atomisation enthalpy.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below.
options: A is correct but R is not correct
Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
A is not correct but R is correct
Both A and R are correct and R is NOT the correct explanation of A
solution: Beryllium has the smallest atomic size amongst the alkaline earth metals. The loss of electrons or oxidation is difficult. Beryllium has the least oxidation potential. As Beryllium has the least oxidation potential, it has the highest reduction potential(Less negative value of reduction potential). However, it's reducing nature is due to large hydration energy associated with the small size of Be2+ ion and relatively large value of the atomisation enthalpy of metal.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Match List I with List II

List I List II
Elements Colour imparted to the flame
A K I Brick Red
B Ca II Violet
C Sr III Apple Green
D Ba IV Crimson Red

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
Match List I with List II

List I List II
Elements Colour imparted to the flame
A K I Brick Red
B Ca II Violet
C Sr III Apple Green
D Ba IV Crimson Red

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution:
The colours of the flame are produced because of the movement of the electrons in the metal ions present in their salts. Calcium exhibits a brick-red colour. Strontium exhibits crimson colour. And the barium exhibits an apple green colour. 

K is an alkali metal. Potassium is colourless or white but when it is heated, it produces a violet flame due to the excitation of the electrons to the higher energy orbitals. Potassium burns with a light purple-coloured flame.

So, the correct option is C

subject: Chemistry
q:
Identify the correct statements about alkali metals.

A. The order of standard reduction potential (M+ | M) for alkali metal ions is Na > Rb > Li.

B. CsI is highly soluble in water.

C. Lithium carbonate is highly stable to heat.

D. Potassium dissolved in concentrated liquid ammonia is blue and paramagnetic.

E. All alkali metal hydrides are ionic solids.

Choose the correct answer from the options given below
question:
Identify the correct statements about alkali metals.

A. The order of standard reduction potential (M+ | M) for alkali metal ions is Na > Rb > Li.

B. CsI is highly soluble in water.

C. Lithium carbonate is highly stable to heat.

D. Potassium dissolved in concentrated liquid ammonia is blue and paramagnetic.

E. All alkali metal hydrides are ionic solids.

Choose the correct answer from the options given below
solution:
A. Electrode potential of given elements are:

 Li = -3.04  Na = -2.714  Rb = -2.98

The standard reduction potentials (E) of the alkali metals do not follow the trend based on ionisation energies. Unexpectedly, lithium is the strongest reductant, and sodium is the weakest. Therefore, reducing character sequences is Na <Rb < Li.

B. The low solubility of CsI is due to smaller hydration enthalpy of its two ions.

C. Li2CO3 decomposes readily to lithium monoxide and carbon dioxide.

D. Alkali metals are sodium, potassium which with liquid ammonia produce blue coloured solutions. Alkali metals dissolve in liquid ammonia to produce ammoniated electrons. It is paramagnetic due to the presence of unpaired electrons.

E.All the alkali metal hydrides are ionic solids with high melting points.

Hence, the correct option is A and E

subject: Chemistry
q: The metal used for making X-ray tube window is:
question: The metal used for making X-ray tube window is:
solution:
Many metals can be used to make the anode of a X-ray tube, but only one metal is most suitable for the windows. It is beryllium because beryllium has lowest electron density outside its nucleus, and will absorb least X-ray.

Beryllium is used in making windows in X-ray tubes.

subject: Chemistry
q: The setting time of Cement is increased by adding
question: The setting time of Cement is increased by adding
solution: Cement or Portland cement mainly contains oxides of calcium, silicon, aluminium, and iron. When water is mixed with cement, the oxides get hydrated, and cement sets extremely fast. To control the setting time of cement, gypsum is added to it, which increases the setting time of cement.

question: Addition of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi>SO</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math> to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>BaO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> produces
solution:
Acidifying barium peroxide and removing excess water by evaporation under reduced pressure gives hydrogen peroxide.

BaO2.8H2Os+H2SO4aq→BaSO4s+H2O2aq+8H2Ol

This is a common method to prepare hydrogen peroxide

subject: Chemistry
q: Chlorides of which metal are soluble in organic solvents:
question: Chlorides of which metal are soluble in organic solvents:
solution:
As per Fajan's rule, Be cations has a higher charge and smaller size, as a result, have a higher charge density and covalent nature.

Ca, Mg, K are ionic compounds.

Hence, chlorides of Be will be more soluble in organic solvent.

subject: Chemistry
q: Equimolar solutions of the following compounds are prepared separately in water. Which will have the lowest $\mathrm{pH}$ value ?
question: Equimolar solutions of the following compounds are prepared separately in water. Which will have the lowest $\mathrm{pH}$ value ?
solution:
Metal halide on hydrolysis with water form corresponding hydroxides.
The basic strength of hydroxide increases as we move down in a group. This is because of the increase in size which results in decrease of ionization energy which weakens the strength of $\mathrm{M}-\mathrm{O}$ bonds in MOH and thus increases the basic strength.
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{Be}(\mathrm{OH})_2 \quad \mathrm{Mg}(\mathrm{OH})_2 \\
& \text { Amphoteric Weak base } \\
& \mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{OH})_2 \mathrm{Sr}(\mathrm{OH})_2 \mathrm{Ba}(\mathrm{OH})_2 \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
Hence, $\mathrm{Be}(\mathrm{OH})_2$ will have lowest $\mathrm{pH}$.

subject: Chemistry
q: For a good quality cement, the ratio of lime to the total of the oxides of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Si</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>Al</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Fe</mi></math> should be as close as to
question: For a good quality cement, the ratio of lime to the total of the oxides of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Si</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>Al</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Fe</mi></math> should be as close as to
solution: Cement is made by strongly heating a mixture of lime stone and clay in a rotatory kiln. Lime stone and clay are finely powdered, and a little water is added. For a good quality cement, the ratio of silica (SiO2) to alumina (Al2O3) should be between 2.5 and 4 and the ratio of lime (CaO) to the total of the oxides of silicon (SiO2) aluminium (Al2O3) and iron (Fe2O3) should be as close as possible to 2. If lime is in excess, the cement cracks during setting. On the other hand, if lime is less than the required, the cement is weak in strength. Therefore, a proper composition of cement must be maintained to get cement of good quality.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Match List I with List II
(A) Slaked lime                                     (I) NaOH
(B) Dead burnt plaster                          (II) Ca(OH)2
(C) Caustic soda                                  (III) Na2CO3·10H2O
(D) Washing soda                                 (IV) CaSO4
Choose the correct answer form the options given below:
question:
Match List I with List II
(A) Slaked lime                                     (I) NaOH
(B) Dead burnt plaster                          (II) Ca(OH)2
(C) Caustic soda                                  (III) Na2CO3·10H2O
(D) Washing soda                                 (IV) CaSO4
Choose the correct answer form the options given below:
solution:
(A) Slaked lime: Calcium oxide is a white amorphous solid. It has a melting point of 2870 K. On exposure to the atmosphere, it absorbs moisture and carbon dioxide. 

CaO + H2O → Ca(OH)2

CaO + CO2 → CaCO3

The addition of a limited amount of water breaks the lump of lime. This process is called slaking of lime.

(B) Dead burnt plaster: Calcium sulphate is a hemihydrate of calcium sulphate. It is obtained when gypsum, CaSO4.2H2O is heated at 393K.

2(CaSO4.2H2O) → 2(CaSO4).H2O + 3H2O

Above 393 K, no water of crystallisation is left and anhydrous calcium sulphate, CaSO4 is formed. This is known as ‘dead burnt plaster’.

(C) Caustic soda: Sodium hydroxide (caustic soda) NaOH is generally prepared commercially by the electrolysis of sodium chloride in Castner-Kellner cell. A brine solution is electrolysed using a mercury cathode and a carbon anode. Sodium metal discharged at the cathode combines with mercury to form sodium amalgam.

(D) Washing soda: Sodium carbonate is a white crystalline solid which exists as a decahydrate, Na2CO3.10H2O. This is also called washing soda. It is readily soluble in water.

So, the correct matching is (A)-II, (B)-IV, (C)-I, (D)-III.

subject: Chemistry
q: The correct order of density is
question: The correct order of density is
solution:
The size of alkali metals increases down the group, so volume also shows increment, and since volume is inversely proportional to the density but in the case Sr and Ba, increase in the volume is less compared to increasing mass.

So, down the group density decreases first and then increases.

Therefore, the correct order of density is as follows:

'Ca<Mg<Be<Sr

q: A metal on combustion in excess air forms <math><mi>X</mi></math> . <math><mi>X</mi></math> upon hydrolysis with water yields <math><msub><mrow><mi>H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><msub><mrow><mi>O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> and <math><msub><mrow><mi>O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> along with another product.
